return.speaker: narrator
return.text: She has not moved from the cairn. The light is different now — long and red on the stones — but her shape is the same, the sword across the same knees. She watches you cross the moor without rising. The watching is its own answer.

settle.speaker: lyra
settle.text: You came back. I half wagered you would not. Sit, then. The stone is cold and the wind is honest. Better company than I have had this season.

story-1.speaker: lyra
story-1.text: There were five of us, on the bog job. I had the rank. I had the orders. The mark we were chasing did not run the way the briefing said he would. He ran toward the water, and the water was not water — it was the kind of bog that does not give a body back.

story-2.speaker: lyra
story-2.text: I called the line back. I called it twice. Bren — my second — went in after the mark anyway. The other three followed her, the way younger blades do. I held the rise alone, because someone had to hold the rise. By the time the bog was done, I had a sword and four names to write down and no one to write them with.

story-3.speaker: lyra
story-3.text: The sergeant on the muster yard listened to my report and said one word. 'Cowardice.' That was all. I gave him my token. I left the yard before he could write the word into the ledger. I have been hunting bandits along this stretch since. The work does not need a token. It does not ask after my name.

story-options.speaker: lyra
story-options.text: So. There it is. The short version, since you asked.

choices.text: Brenna spoke well of you. She did not use that word.
[Say nothing. Let her hear her own silence.]

forgiven.speaker: lyra
forgiven.text: ...Brenna said that. Out loud. To you. Hm. She always was the only one on that yard who counted the names before she counted the win. I will not call it forgiveness. I will call it weight off a stone I did not know I was carrying.

forgiven-2.speaker: lyra
forgiven-2.text: If you have honest work and a road that needs a second blade — find me here again. I am not promising a yes. I am promising I will hear the question. That is more than I have given anyone in a year.

silence.speaker: lyra
silence.text: ...Aye. Silence is the right answer too. There are stories that ask for it. Walk on, then. The cairn keeps me. It will keep me a while yet.
